channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Politics Friday: Democrats seek a new leader with Minnesota’s DFL party chair in contention
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor and host Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the Democratic National Committee’s election of a new party chair and the Minnesotan who’s campaigning for the job. Later, a conversation with newly elected Minnesota Republican Party Chair Alex Plechash.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump's FBI chief pick, Kash Patel, insists he has no 'enemies list' and won't seek retribution
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, insisted to deeply skeptical Democrats on Thursday that he did not have an “enemies list" and that the bureau under his leadership would not seek retribution against the president's adversaries or launch investigations for
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Fate of Trump’s Cabinet hangs in the balance following 3 more confirmation hearings
item.description: Three of President Donald Trump’s cabinet picks prepared to face skepticism and intense grilling from Democratic senators during their confirmation hearings Thursday. What we’re following: 1. Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames diversity hiring as probe into deadly midair collision begins
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the nation reeled from the deadliest American aviation disaster in more than two decades, President Donald Trump on Thursday baselessly blamed diversity initiatives for undermining air safety and questioned the actions of a U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DEI drops and House drama won't stop Black Entrepreneurs Day at the Capitol
item.description: Activist Sheletta Brundidge says the third annual Black Entrepreneurs Day, which is scheduled for Monday, will go off without a hitch, despite being unexpectedly short of its $25,000 price tag.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. on defensive over his vaccine views as a key confirmation vote hangs in the balance
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to be the nation's top health official is uncertain after a key Republican joined Democrats to raise persistent concerns over the nominee's deep skepticism of routine childhood vaccinations that prevent deadly diseases. Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kansas lawmakers vow to find answers after Wichita plane crash in DC
item.description: House and Senate lawmakers from Kansas released a bipartisan statement on the tragic American Airlines crash Wednesday night in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats press Army secretary nominee if ‘readiness’ affected by southern border deployments
item.description: Democrats sounded off about the White House sending U.S. troops to the southern border, but Army secretary nominee Daniel Driscoll insisted that he did not believe it would affect readiness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Risch proposes bill to block US foreign aid from funding abortions
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Senate Foreign Relations Committee Chairman Jim Risch and GOP senators on Thursday are expected to roll out a measure that would prohibit the use of U.S. foreign aid funds for abortions, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Kash Patel hammers ‘grotesque mischaracterizations’ from Dems amid fiery FBI confirmation hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Donald Trump's pick to lead the FBI, furiously hit back at what he described as "grotesque mischaracterizations" from Democrats at his confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump consoles crash victims then dives into politics with attack on diversity initiatives
item.description: At a news conference on Thursday, President Donald Trump veered into politics to speculate that Democrats and diversity initiatives could be to blame for the deaths of 67 people in the incident.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Graham grills FBI nominee Patel over 'disgusting' and 'corrupt' Crossfire Hurricane probe
item.description: FBI director nominee Kash Patel was grilled Thursday over the FBI’s investigation into alleged Trump-Russia connection, known as "Crossfire Hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blames DEI for weakening FAA in aftermath of Reagan National plane crash
item.description: President Trump on Thursday appeared to blame diversity initiatives at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for weakening safety following a deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Gabbard makes case to lead national intelligence at confirmation hearing
item.description: Former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (D-Hawaii) is testifying Thursday morning before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence to make her case as President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Trump FBI agent responsible for opening Jack Smith elector case against president: whistleblower
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: A previously identified anti-Trump FBI agent allegedly broke protocol and played a critical role in opening and advancing the bureau’s original investigation related to the 2020 election, tying President Trump to the probe without sufficient predication.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance suggests some reporters covering the Trump White House are biased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President JD Vance is suggesting that some reporters covering the Trump White House are biased — perhaps even Democratic “propagandists” — and the new administration will keep a close eye on those asking questions at press briefings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump World learns how to deal with Musk
item.description: President Trump’s allies and aides are learning to deal with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has had a consistent presence in the early days of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sparks expected to fly at Kash Patel’s Senate confirmation hearing to lead FBI
item.description: Kash Patel is set to join the Senate Judiciary Committee on Thursday morning to face his nomination hearing to serve as the second Trump administration's FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math, according to the latest results of an exam known as the nation’s report card. The findings are yet another setback for U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right health recommendations
item.description: The latest KFF survey finds 42 percent of respondents say they have either a great deal or fair amount of trust in Trump to make the right recommendations when it comes to health issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Bob Menendez sentenced to 11 years in prison following first-of-its-kind conviction
item.description: Former Sen. Bob Menendez (D-N.J.) was sentenced Wednesday to 11 years in prison for trading bribes for political favors.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez embraces role as attack dog against Trump
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez is embracing her role as one of the party's sharpest critics of President Trump, while other Democrats are working on a softer approach, with Ocasio-Cortez's actions showing her as the party's most aggressive member.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Doron Clark wins Senate special election, DFLers take back the Minnesota Senate
item.description: DFLer Doron Clark won in the deep-blue Minneapolis district with 90 percent of the vote, according to unofficial results.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Public confidence in US health agencies declines, survey says
item.description: Trust in health institutions tended to fall along party lines, with Democrats showing greater support.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Former Congresswoman Shelley Berkley settles in her new role as Las Vegas mayor
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Mayor Shelley Berkley is settling into her new role after decades of Goodman leadership in Las Vegas. The former congresswoman now holds the title of mayor.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Uptick in ICE operations will soon strain resources: Ex-director
item.description: "Do we have the bed space? We're going to require a lot more resources and money to do that," said former ICE Director Patrick J. Lechleitner.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Deb Haaland made history as Interior secretary. Now she’s running for governor of New Mexico
item.description: Deb Haaland, who championed conservation and clean energy during her tenure as Interior secretary, is running for the Democratic nomination for governor in New Mexico, the nation's No. 2 oil production state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Republicans pass Democrats in voter registration, PAC reports
item.description: Nevada Republicans said Monday they have passed Democrats in voter registration in the state for the first time since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump addresses House Republicans at Florida retreat: 5 takeaways
item.description: DORAL, Fla. — President Trump floated running for a third term, commented on a new Chinese artificial intelligence app, and urged House Republicans to unify and quickly advance his legislative priorities in a speech to the lawmakers at their annual policy retreat.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: NJ congressman launches plan to tackle rising meat and egg prices
item.description: FORT LEE, N.J. (PIX11) -- Food prices in the coming year are expected to continue to increase, according to the Department of Agriculture. It's unlikely to be the sharp rise we saw in the past few years, but certain products such as eggs and meats continue to rise more than most other groceries.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
